Antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) is fundamental to both individual patient management and population-level surveillance. @tbl-resistance-test-rate presents the rate at which pathogens isolated from infections undergo testing for certain relevant antimicrobial resistances. This section assesses testing *completeness*`r if (nzchar(ref_abr_table)) "; @tbl-abr-infection-rate reports the detected resistant pathogens themselves." else "."` High testing rates ensure reliable data for antimicrobial stewardship programs and enable accurate assessment of resistance trends. Lower rates may indicate gaps in microbiological diagnostic capacity or protocols that could limit your ability to detect and respond to emerging resistance patterns.
